notes: Issued by Emperor Meiji, 30 October 1890. Public domain. Standard English translation published by the Japanese Ministry of Education. Widely reproduced in primary-source collections.
Issued by Emperor Meiji, 4 January 1882. Public domain. English translation widely reproduced in scholarship on Japanese militarism.
Used as theory background; no copyrighted full text committed.
Passage text cited from Baynes (1942) edition; requires verification against that or equivalent scholarly edition before advancing past source-review.
Public domain party programme. Point 10 directly encodes collective priority over individual activity. Text widely reproduced and stable across editions.
Official English translation, Deutscher Bundestag, 2012 ed. Art. 1(1) and Art. 79(3) (eternity clause) are the key passages for this chain.

First government declaration to the Bundestag, 20 September 1949. Corroborates the constitutional-containment reading by showing the new symbolic order's explicit rejection of the Nazi-era collective-body logic at the moment of the Federal Republic's founding.
Delivered 25 December 1991, broadcast on Soviet state television. English translation widely reproduced; passage text requires verification against a scholarly edition.
Signed 21 December 1991 by eleven former Soviet republics. Official governmental document; public domain. English text reproduced in major international law collections.
Public Law 93-148, enacted 7 November 1973. United States federal law; public domain. Full text in US Statutes at Large 87 Stat. 555 and codified at 50 U.S.C. §§ 1541-1548.
Address by President Jimmy Carter, 15 July 1979. Public domain US government document. Archived at the Jimmy Carter Presidential Library and Miller Center, University of Virginia.
